rationale: The revision closes the original branch-probe fail-open in intent: BranchExistsE feeds the error-preserving core, all four R1 infrastructure legs and both R3 legs are fail-closed, advisory slot naming alone is fail-open, hatches do not bypass obligations, and the two residuals remain honestly scoped. But the new seam leaves a concrete false-refusal ambiguity. Current `git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<missing>` exits 128; if BranchExistsE merely preserves that error as specified, every normally deleted bead branch becomes an infrastructure failure and impl approve refuses. The spec never requires genuine absence to return `(false, nil)` or pins that behavior in gitutil/approve tests. It also does not pin mixed-list wrapper parity: an error-preserving scan that aborts on one bead and a wrapper that swallows the scan error returns no later provable orphans, unlike today's per-bead skip-and-continue behavior. Both are branch-fix residues. AC discriminators are RED, h4n5 is persisted, AC7 is cycle-safe, AC10 is exactly-one, and item 7 is properly disposed.
concrete_changes_required: Define BranchExistsE's three-way contract explicitly: present => (true, nil), genuinely absent => (false, nil), and transient/structural git failure => non-nil error. Ground the distinction using the existing RevParseRef/ErrRefNotFound exit-1-versus-exit-128 precedent (or an equally reliable primitive).
Add a named gitutil test for present/absent/infra outcomes and extend AC2 or AC1 with an ApproveImpl deleted-branch normal-path test proving a legitimately absent bead branch does not refuse.

Pin byte-identical FindOrphanedClosedBeads behavior with a mixed closed-bead list: a branch-probe or ancestry error for one bead must still allow later provable orphans to be returned, while ScanOrphanedClosedBeads reports the error and the impl-approve gate refuses.

findings.note: The fourth fail-closed leg is added, but genuine ref absence is not distinguished from probe failure and the fail-open wrapper's per-bead skip-and-continue parity is not pinned, creating false-refusal/regression risk.
AC1-AC7 now use exact named-test existence discriminators; all named tests and the AC4 ScanOrphanedClosedBeads anchor are absent at 77d8a1a9, so the proofs are RED.
AC7 is split between package-local complete coverage and an approve post-settlement test, avoiding complete -> approve -> complete.
Branchless-obligation recovery now requires restoring bead/<id> before complete, or out-of-band settlement; AC6 asserts the prerequisite.
mindspec-h4n5 appears exactly once in the pinned .beads/issues.jsonl.
AC10 enumerates all domain ownership manifests and requires workflow to be the sole lifecycle claimant.
cmd/** is dropped from declared scope and the execution self-ownership gap is explicitly evidenced as pre-existing and out of scope.
